review_content.General: I'm from the 2017 batch. This college is good for practice purposes and knowledge. It is good with opportunities to learn new things. Labs are good. Ras bhashajya (aushadhi nirman) labs are well developed and provide good knowledge. Nice and healthy environment for students.
review_content.Infrastructure: Now classrooms are made with screens for PPT presentations. Classrooms are well developed. Hostel is yet to be renovated that is an old building near the college for girls. College is at the backside at jatharperh akola on station road. There are two hospitals gmc akola and phc where internships are done.

reviews_list.batch_info: BAMS - Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery - Batch of 2028
reviews_list.review_title: Campus is very small, but has two hospitals, and quite a decent patient inflow.

review_content.Placements: People not getting MBBS get this course and our college alloted students with decent ranks. There is 1-year internship after 4.5 years of B.A.M.S. course. There are many branches to do your PG in B.A.M.S. ahead. You can do various types of surgeries now by taking a seat for MS in PG through a competitive exam.
review_content.Infrastructure: Talking about the infrastructure, our college campus is quite small. There is no space for sports, etc. But has 2 hospitals in it which leads to more patient inflow, hostels could be better, and canteen is not present. Labs are present in every department and practices are also done according to the schedule provided.

reviews_list.batch_info: BAMS - Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery - Batch of 2027
reviews_list.review_title: Overall good college with average facilities.

review_content.Placements: The college is a government ayurvedic college. They offer Rs.18,000 stipend/month currently. The salary offered in our course after graduation is around Rs.40,000-60,000 per month depending on the skills you have. Private and government hospitals offer jobs in our course. One can get decent government sector jobs or open own clinic.

overview_faqs.question: Is Radhakisan Toshniwal Ayurved Mahavidyalaya a known college/Institute?
overview_faqs.answer: Radhakisan Toshniwal Ayurved Mahavidyalaya is a distinguished institution offering MD, B.A.M.S. Students can choose these programs to launch their careers in the Medicine & Health Sciences field. Radhakisan Toshniwal Ayurved Mahavidyalaya is known for its dedicated and experienced faculty members committed to delivering these courses. Radhakisan Toshniwal Ayurved Mahavidyalaya has received an impressive review rating of 3.7 based on student feedback at Shiksha. Additionally, it has earned ratings of 4 for Campus Life, 3.5 for Placements, 4 for Faculty, and 3 for Infrastructure.

review_summary.college_name: Radhakisan Toshniwal Ayurved Mahavidyalaya Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: BAMS - Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery - Batch of 2022
individual_reviews.title: Our college is excellent with best education facilities.

content.Placements: Many students start their OPD and some of them pursue post-graduation from renowned universities. Lots of options are available after this course. Ayurvedic pharmaceutical is also the field where you can launch the best and most genuine ayurvedic products. Medical officer, and research officer are the roles offered.
content.Infrastructure: Hostel facilities are available only for girls. No canteen facilities are available. The college infrastructure is good enough. The best thing about college is the library. College has an attached hospital. Research and panchakarma center is also available here.

individual_reviews.course: BAMS - Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery - Batch of 2023
individual_reviews.title: Our college is good for knowledge.

content.Placements: I'm from the 2017 batch. This college is good for practice purposes and knowledge. It is good with opportunities to learn new things. Labs are good. Ras bhashajya (aushadhi nirman) labs are well developed and provide good knowledge. Nice and healthy environment for students.
content.Infrastructure: Now classrooms are made with screens for PPT presentations. Classrooms are well developed. Hostel is yet to be renovated that is an old building near the college for girls. College is at the backside at jatharperh akola on station road. There are two hospitals gmc akola and phc where internships are done.

individual_reviews.course: BAMS - Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Campus is very small, but has two hospitals, and quite a decent patient inflow.

content.Placements: People not getting MBBS get this course and our college alloted students with decent ranks. There is 1-year internship after 4.5 years of B.A.M.S. course. There are many branches to do your PG in B.A.M.S. ahead. You can do various types of surgeries now by taking a seat for MS in PG through a competitive exam.
content.Infrastructure: Talking about the infrastructure, our college campus is quite small. There is no space for sports, etc. But has 2 hospitals in it which leads to more patient inflow, hostels could be better, and canteen is not present. Labs are present in every department and practices are also done according to the schedule provided.

individual_reviews.course: BAMS - Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery - Batch of 2027
individual_reviews.title: Overall good college with average facilities.

content.Placements: The college is a government ayurvedic college. They offer Rs.18,000 stipend/month currently. The salary offered in our course after graduation is around Rs.40,000-60,000 per month depending on the skills you have. Private and government hospitals offer jobs in our course. One can get decent government sector jobs or open own clinic.
content.Infrastructure: The college building infrastructure is quite average. It has no boys hostel currently and the girls hostel has been renovated recently. The classrooms have whiteboards, projectors, and other basic facilities accommodating 60-70 students. Some of the departments like anatomy are in bad condition with no proper cadaveric dissection for first-year students. Other departments in the first and second are in average condition. No canteen is on the campus, the library is available with academic books and is in good condition.

college_info.intro:
Sabarmati University popular programmes are BSc and MBA. BSc students are placed in top companies in top sectors. BSc is provided through various departments such as Maths, Computer & Informative Sciences, Physics & Astronomical Sciences, and others. BSc students can work as Operations Researcher and can enter in fields such as Data Science, Actuarial Science, Teaching, and others. Sabarmati University is one of the reputed universities that is visited by top companies such as Reliance Retail, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Accenture, and others.
Overall, Sabarmati University is one of the top-ranked universities present in India. Over the years the university has received various awards. MBA students can further plan to seek higher education or can work as Relationship Managers at banks/NBFCs, Credit Analysts, Investment Bankers, Financial Analysts, Finance Managers, and others. Below mentioned are some of the programmes offered by Sabarmati University and their respective eligibility and Sabarmati University fee structure:

faqs.question: How are the placements at Sabarmati University?

faqs.answer: A:    After the completion of a BA from Sabarmati University, candidates can apply for different government jobs according to their specialisation. Graduates tend to seek employment in public administration, teaching, and social services. Candidates can apply for competitive exams such as UPSC, SSC, and State Public Service Commissions for the positions of IAS, IPS, or other government departments. Besides, there are opportunities in banking, railways, and other PSUs, where a BA degree is a qual

faqs.answer: A:    Yes, completing a BSc course from Sabarmati University makes students eligible for job opportunities in the public sector of India. The Indian government sector recruits students with knowledge of Computer Science, IT, Mathematics, etc..,  in various capacities. Students who wish to work in reputed public organisations can explore jobs in BHEL, ISRO, GAIL, and more. Fresh graduates are generally given entry-level positions, such as Research Assistant, Jr. Developer, Software Engineer and Q

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharm.) - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: There are many other good and newly developed buildings and branches in this university.

content.Placements: Most students got placements through this college. The lowest package was 3 LPA, and the highest was 18 LPA. Companies such as Cadila, Mankind, Intas, and Zydus, which are MNCs, participated. Around 30% of students got placed at Zydus, 40% at Cadila, 10% at Intas, and 20% at Mankind. All these roles were offered in this college.
content.Infrastructure: The college had a big AC hall, a library, and Wi-Fi all over the campus. It had neat and clean smart classrooms, parking, a canteen, sitting areas, and more. There were good PG and hostels within 1km of the university, all of which provided pickup and drop-off facilities whenever students had to go to the university. The university timings were from 10am to 2pm.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Computer Application (BCA) - Batch of 2016
individual_reviews.title: Teachers are good here. The college offers a good environment.

content.Placements: The college doesn't provide placements for the students. However, the knowledge provided by them was useful to get a job after graduation. The college doesn't provide internships for the students. Students have to find internships on their own. There is no placement cell in the college.
content.Infrastructure: The college has very good infrastructure including Wi-Fi, sports complex, shuttle service, library, computer and psychology lab. The quality of the classrooms is very good. The college provides separate hostels for boys and girls, which is 6 km away from the campus. Hostels are equipped with every necessary facility like a recreation room, sports complex, etc. Hostels rooms are very clean and comfortable. I didn't face any difficulties regarding the infrastructure of the college during my tenure.

individual_reviews.course: M.Sc. in Chemistry - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: The Sabarmati University.

content.Placements: Every year placement teams visit our campus, and they give the best placements to students. In my course, last year 57 students were placed by the college in the best companies. So they give a guarantee of 100% placement, and that's true also. So if you're going, don't worry about placement.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of the college is very good. On-campus, a study environment is provided. My college is the place where I used to spend most of my time. I love that place a lot, and I have lots of friends there. Whenever we get leisure time in the college period, we hang out on the campus. All the teachers are qualified and experienced. They teach us with great care and love.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Computer Application (BCA) - Batch of 2018
individual_reviews.title: Review of Sabarmati University.

content.Placements: Most of students are placed in my course. In my course, the highest package was 12 lakh per annum and the lowest was, I don't know, I forgot, I think it is 2 lakh per annum. No big companies came to the college. Most of students were doing the internships.
content.Infrastructure: Facilities are there in my course, like Wi-Fi, labs, AC classrooms, but no library. In my college, no canteen, no mess, no sports, no games, no medical facilities are there. And there was not even a good campus. I did not enjoy my college life.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Arts (B.A.) - Batch of 2016
individual_reviews.title: Amazing experience under good guidance.

content.Placements: As it was just a B.A. programme, no such placements were there. Students surely went for higher courses but no internships were required to do so but students were well prepared and mentally ready to work for any MNC and we're encouraged to go for higher studies to achieve the required qualifications.
content.Infrastructure: The college had its hostel, library, and labs as well. There were play grounds as well including a badminton court, a basketball court. Quality of food was much better than that offered in Delhi. The food was as good as any restaurant but simple.

admission_overview.title: Sabarmati University Admission Overview 2025
admission_overview.description: Sabarmati University (formerly Calorx Teachers' University) offers 18 UG, 10 PG, Diploma, PG Diploma, MPhil and PhD programmes in a regular mode such as BBA, BCom, BA, BCA, B.Ed, LLB, MSc, etc. as well as eight UG and four PG courses under the School of Distance Learning like BA, MA and BCom. It also offers three-year BVoc courses in nine subjects such as Automobile Engineering, Beauty, Salon and Spa, Media and Mass Communication, Tourism and Hospitality, among others with three exit points, namely a certificate (at the end of six months), diploma (at the end of one year) and BVoc degree (at the end of three years). Admission to all the courses offered by Sabarmati University is merit-based in the last qualifying examination, followed by a GD/ PI round, as applicable

The alumni of the University are currently working with TCS, Accenture, Tech Mahindra, and other renowned companies .

faqs.question: Which are the top companies taking part in the placements drive at Sabarmati University?
faqs.answer: The top companies taking part in the placements drive at Sabarmati University include Vodafone, Aditya Birla Group, Google, Honda, Samsung, TCS (Tata Consultancy Services), Bajaj Allianz, etc. These are some of the companies that have visited Sabarmati University for recruitment. Not as many companies come to Sabarmati University for oncampus placements. One of the best things is that the chance of offcampus placement is very high because they provide 100 percents assistance for placement to all its registered students.

faqs.question: Are there any government job opportunities that I can seek after completing BSc from Sabarmati University?
faqs.answer: Yes, completing a BSc course from Sabarmati University makes students eligible for job opportunities in the public sector of India. The Indian government sector recruits students with knowledge of Computer Science, IT, Mathematics, etc.., in various capacities. Students who wish to work in reputed public organisations can explore jobs in BHEL, ISRO, GAIL, and more. Fresh graduates are generally given entry-level positions, such as Research Assistant, Jr. Developer, Software Engineer and Quality Analyst . The starting salary offered to fresh graduates working in the public sector ranges from INR 4 LPA to INR 6 LPA .

reviews.review_heading: There are many other good and newly developed buildings and branches in this university.
reviews.review_text: The college had a big AC hall, a library, and Wi-Fi all over the campus. It had neat and clean smart classrooms, parking, a canteen, sitting areas, and more. There were good PG and hostels within 1km of the university, all of which provided pickup and drop-off facilities whenever students had to go to the university. The university timings were from 10am to 2pm.

reviews.review_heading: Teachers are good here. The college offers a good environment.
reviews.review_text: The college has very good infrastructure including Wi-Fi, sports complex, shuttle service, library, computer and psychology lab. The quality of the classrooms is very good. The college provides separate hostels for boys and girls, which is 6 km away from the campus. Hostels are equipped with every necessary facility like a recreation room, sports complex, etc. Hostels rooms are very clean and comfortable. I didn't face any difficulties regarding the infrastructure of the college during my tenure...

reviews.review_heading: The Sabarmati University.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of the college is very good. On-campus, a study environment is provided. My college is the place where I used to spend most of my time. I love that place a lot, and I have lots of friends there. Whenever we get leisure time in the college period, we hang out on the campus. All the teachers are qualified and experienced. They teach us with great care and love.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Sabarmati University.
reviews.review_text:
Facilities are there in my course, like Wi-Fi, labs, AC classrooms, but no library.
In my college, no canteen, no mess, no sports, no games, no medical facilities are there.
And there was not even a good campus. I did not enjoy my college life.

reviews.review_heading: Amazing experience under good guidance.
reviews.review_text: The college had its hostel, library, and labs as well. There were play grounds as well including a badminton court, a basketball court. Quality of food was much better than that offered in Delhi. The food was as good as any restaurant but simple.

faqs.question: Which entrance exam is accepted at KIIT School Of Public Health MHA?
faqs.answer: In order to qualify for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions, candidates must appear for the KIITEE conducted by KIIT Deemed to be University. The institute does not accept the scores of any other entrance exams to admit students into the MHA course.Candidates must fulfill the eligibility criteria of the MHA course to secure their admission at KIIT School Of Public Health.

faqs.question: Does KIIT School Of Public Health accept NEET-PG scores during admission to MHA?
faqs.answer: No, KIIT School Of Public Health does not accept NEET-PG scores when selecting candidates for the MHA course. Only the scores obtained in the KIITEE will be considered for choosing candidates for the MHA course at KIIT School Of Public Health MHA.Candidates who have a valid NEET-PG score will need to registe for the aforementioned KIITEE to apply for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions.

faqs.question: Is KIITEE registration open for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions?
faqs.answer: Yes, the KIITEE registrations for the academic year 2026 are open for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions. Currently, the first phase of KIITEE registrations is ongoing at KIIT Deemed to be University. The last date to apply for the first session of KIITEE 2026 is scheduled on April 8th.

faqs.question: When will the registrations for KIITEE end for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions?
faqs.answer: The final deadline for KIITEE registrations for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions is scheduled on July 1st for the academic year 2026.The aforementioned date is the last date to apply for the third and final round of KIITEE 2026. Candidates who miss this deadline will need to wait for KIITEE 2027 registrations to begin for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions.

faqs.question: What are the eligibility criteria for admission at KIIT School of Public Health?
faqs.answer: The eligibility criteria for admission at KIIT School of Public Health have been discussed below:·Candidate must have Bachelors’ degree in Medicine, AYUSH,Nursing, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Veterinary Sciences, Physiotherapy, or Allied Health Sciences from a recognised Institute / University with a minimum 50% marks in aggregate (or)·Bachelor’s degree in Technology (BTech),Public Health (BPH),Business Administration (BBA) or Law (LLB) from a recognised Institute / University with minimum 50% marks in aggregate, (or)·Bachelor’s degree in Science / Life Science / Statistics /Economics/ Nutrition / Demography from a recognised Institute / University with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate, (or)·Post-graduation degree inPsychology, Anthropology or Sociology or Equivalent Degree from a recognised Institute / University with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate.

faqs.question: Is direct admission possible in MHA at KIIT School Of Public Health?
faqs.answer: No, candidates cannot get direct admission into the KIIT School Of Public Health MHA course. Candidates who want to apply for MHA admissions at KIIT School Of Public Health must appear for the KIITEE first.The selection will be based on the candidate's performance in the aforementioned entrance exam.

faqs.question: Is work experience mandatory for KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions?
faqs.answer: No, the KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions do not require candidates to have prior experience to be eligible. However, candidates who do have work experience before applying for MHA admissions will be given preference over other candidates.Only work experience in the public health domain will be counted towards KIIT School Of Public Health MHA admissions.

Each hostel has 24-hour reading room, the facility for indoor and outdoor games and wireless internet and intranet connectivity. There is a dedicated international hostel accommodates foreign students.

library.description: The library is stocked with over 3,08,071 books, 6806 bound volumes, 553 current subscriptions to print journals, magazines, newspapers, and many other resources like student's project reports, CDs (2813), videos (555) and etc. ICT services support the library in order to provide a wide range of IT services to facilitate the works of staffs, students, and faculties.

cafeteria.description: There are 15 food courts specialized in cuisines from different parts of the world. They serve a wide selection of national of national and international dishes. These food courts are in addition to 20 canteens attached to as many hostels.

hostel.description: There are 20 hostels for boys and 11 hostels for girls are available with accommodation capacity of 10180 and 5648 students respectively. About 30% of the hostel rooms are air-conditioned and all the hostels have attached canteens serving good quality food. Each hostel has 24-hour reading room, the facility for indoor and outdoor games and wireless internet and intranet connectivity. There is a dedicated international hostel accommodates foreign students.
